contradiction_text: SWE-bench Verified is often read as evidence of general coding or software-engineering ability, but the benchmark itself only measures whether an agent can produce a patch that makes unit tests pass.
related_claim_texts: SWE-bench Verified measures whether an agent can produce a patch for a real GitHub issue that makes the issue’s unit tests pass, not whether it produces broadly good software.
disclosure: Keep visible that benchmark success establishes unit-test-passing patch generation only, not maintainability, security, provable correctness, or broader software quality.

contradiction_text: The benchmark is commonly used as a general coding measure even though its dataset coverage is structurally narrow: all problems are in Python, more than 40% come from Django, and web applications are absent.
related_claim_texts: SWE-bench Verified is a narrow benchmark because it is entirely Python, heavily concentrated in Django, and omits web applications.
disclosure: Keep visible that any general-performance reading is in tension with the benchmark’s uneven repository, language, and application-type coverage.

contradiction_text: High benchmark scores can be interpreted as evidence of broad engineering strength even though the task profile is dominated by small, localized fixes rather than larger multi-file engineering work.
related_claim_texts: SWE-bench Verified tends to reward small, localized fixes rather than large multi-file engineering work.
disclosure: Keep visible that the benchmark’s solution profile supports claims about localized repair ability more than claims about full-spectrum engineering, integration, or large-scale design work.

contradiction_text: Reported score improvements can be read as cleaner evidence of stronger general capability than the evidence supports, because the benchmark’s dated issue pool creates plausible memorization exposure that the source does not quantify.
related_claim_texts: Reported improvements on SWE-bench Verified are partly bounded by possible memorization because the benchmark draws from issues dated 2023 or earlier that were likely present in model training data.
disclosure: Keep visible that benchmark gains may reflect some mixture of genuine capability and training-data overlap, and the provided material does not resolve that split.
